title: [windows] Podman won't run an image if known_hosts in .ssh does not exist.
body:
### Issue Description

This has happened on 2 fresh Windows 11 installations. Trying to run an image with this command (after installing podman desktop and all things from onboarding without error):

```pwsh
podman run --rm `
  --name pgvectors `
  -e POSTGRES_USER=postgres `
  -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=flyingsquirrel `
  -e POSTGRES_DB=photos `
  -p 5432:5432 `
  -v pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data `
  -d tensorchord/pgvecto-rs:pg17-v0.4.0
```

I get this error:

```
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: open C:\Users\Ruurd\.ssh\known_hosts: The system cannot find the path specified.
```

Trying to fix it, I first tried `podman machine init`, `podman machine start` & `podman system connection list`:

```
PS C:\Users\Ruurd\PycharmProjects\photos-backend> podman machine init
Error: podman-machine-default: VM already exists
PS C:\Users\Ruurd\PycharmProjects\photos-backend> podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Error: machine podman-machine-default: VM already running or starting
PS C:\Users\Ruurd\PycharmProjects\photos-backend> podman system connection list
Name                         URI                                                          Identity
                                 Default     ReadWrite
podman-machine-default       ssh://user@127.0.0.1:53323/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock  C:\Users\Ruurd\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\machine  false       true
podman-machine-default-root  ssh://root@127.0.0.1:53323/run/podman/podman.sock            C:\Users\Ruurd\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\machine  true        true
```

I was able to fix it by manually creating an empty known hosts file in .ssh (the folder didnt exist yet before hand). It works as expected after that.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. On windows, don't have a ~/.ssh/known_hosts file
2. Try to run an image with podman
3. See the error


### Describe the results you received

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: open C:\Users\Ruurd\.ssh\known_hosts: The system cannot find the path specified.

### Describe the results you expected

It should work without me having to make an empty known_hosts file.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Fresh windows install.

### Additional information

Something interesting I just noticed, if I delete the known_hosts file after it has worked once, the known_hosts file *is* created when running podman. So i'm guessing it only happens if podman has never ran successfully before or something.
